svg.text: SVD++ recommender pipeline - customer_id 235 (Dustin Dwayne Diaz)
Numerics: committed recommender/models/surprise_model.pkl (Surprise SVD++ on recommender/data/train_ratings.csv)

g.text: 1) Bookings (concept + shipped CSV)
Live: Booking rows + BookingAddon rows (session_status in Ongoing, BOOKED).
ETL: merged_bookings.csv + booking_addons.csv - customer 235 has 3 rows in shipped merged (Delight, Ecstasy, Allure).
Your five-session story includes Mar 2026 Adore; that row is not in shipped merged - month table below uses 2026-03 anyway to match that scenario.
Addons in history (train data): Delight + addon::7; Ecstasy + addon::9; Allure + addon::5, addon::12 (implicit rating 1 each).
Implicit matrix: one row per (user_id, item_id) with rating in [0,1] here always 1 for positives.
2) Train Surprise SVD++ (recommender/trainer.py pattern)
Reader(rating_scale=(0,1)); build_full_trainset(); SVDpp.fit - learns mu, user biases bu, item biases bi,
latent factors p_u, q_i, and implicit y_j for items in each user implicit neighborhood (SVD++ paper).
Predict: est(u,i) used by recommender/loader.predict_est for package ids (e.g. "2.0") and addons as "addon::id".
Trainset here: 1397 users, 32 items (packages + addon tokens coarsely).
3) recommend_for_user (recommender/loader.py)
load_popularity_tables() - month_package_popularity, month_addon_popularity, month_package_addon_cooccurrence.
prepare_month_tables_with_fallback(month="2026-03") - candidate packages for that month: ["17.0","1.0","2.0","5.0"] (counts 2,1,1,1).
is_known_user(235) = True - skip popularity-only path.
For each candidate package p: score0 = predict_est(235,p) + get_user_package_history_pct(235,p).
Shipped merged_bookings.csv: hist 1/3 for packages 1.0, 2.0, 3.0; March pool has 1.0,2.0 so both get +0.333333 boost.
alpha=0.6, MAX_ADDONS_PER_PACKAGE=4, MARGINAL_THRESHOLD=0.01, SIZE_PENALTY_MULT=0.25.
4) Greedy addons per package (subset_score_for_user)
subset_score = 0.6*pkg_pred + 0.4*sum(addon_pred) + 0.001*sum(package_addon_cooc) - 0.25*num_addons + hist_pct
Candidate order for package 2.0 (Delight): ["2","7","8","11","6","34"] from coocc + global addon popularity.
Package 2.0 greedy (marginal gain > 0.01): start 0.933333; pick addon 2 (+0.151) -> 1.084333;
Final decomposition (2.0, addons [2,7,8,6]): pkg_pred=1, addon_preds each 1, cooc sum=3,
Ecstasy 1.0 greedy yields [2,6,7,8] with score 1.5177350282057984; Adore 17.0 yields [34,2,6,7] at 1.201.
Sort three bundles by score: Delight 1.536333, Ecstasy 1.517735, Adore 1.201.
Top bundle: package_id 2.0 (Delight) + addon ids [2, 7, 8, 6]
Map labels in your copy: history used addon::7 (e.g. soft copies); model picks different high-est ids under this seed.
5) Popularity fallback (cold user or missing model)
If algo is None OR not is_known_user: _popularity_fallback(pkg_pop, addon_pop, coocc, top_k).
Example: user_id 99999999, month 2026-03 returns source=popularity_fallback
recommender/service.py: live Django path can return history-based recs before CF; loader path is the SVD++ + greedy stack above.
Re-run: python -m recommender.loader --user 235 --month 2026-03 (same loader + pickle).

svg.text: SMIL steps replay on refresh. Numbers tied to shipped CSV artifacts + surprise_model.pkl in repo.
